alchemist-bench.name: an alchemist's bench
alchemist-bench.description: Glass coils and a cold burner await reagents.

forge.name: a smith's forge
forge.description: A banked coal forge with anvil and tongs, warm against the abyssal chill.

hearth.name: the inn's hearth
hearth.description: A great stone hearth banked with glowing coals, a soot-blackened cauldron swinging on an iron arm above the flames and a row of spits laid across the embers. Maeve's broth-pot is never quite empty, and there is always room for a delver to cook their own catch.

lamp.name: an iron lamp
lamp.description: A heavy iron lamp bolted to the rock above the shaft, fed by a slow reservoir of oil. A lever on its side turns the flame up or down — a last light before the descent.

witchglow-cluster.name: a witchglow cluster
witchglow-cluster.description: A great fan of luminous fungus spreads across the wet rock, breathing a steady, cold green light into the vault. Its glow is faint but constant — enough to keep the dark-dwellers at bay.

narrow-fissure.name: a narrow fissure
narrow-fissure.description: A jagged crack splits the rock overhead, narrow as a blade. A thin grey thread of daylight falls through it, and the wind in the gap carries the echo of distant bat-cries.

dripping-pool.name: a dark seep
dripping-pool.description: Water gathers in a shallow, black pool, fed drop by drop from the stone above. It looks cold and clean enough to drink, if you dared.

bone-pile.name: a bone pile
bone-pile.description: A reeking midden of gnawed bones heaped against the wall — rat, and not only rat. A cracked helm and a delver's splintered femur lie among them.

iron-vein.name: an iron vein
iron-vein.description: A seam of rust-red ore threads the broken rock, dense enough to work loose with a steady arm and time. The seam gives up only so much before it runs thin, and needs a long while to be worth the swing again.

smelter.name: a smelting furnace
smelter.description: A squat stone furnace with a clay crucible, kept hot by the row's shared bellows. Ore goes in raw and runs out in bars — the first step from rock to blade.

makers-mark.name: a worn maker's mark
makers-mark.description: Scratched low on a workbench leg, half-rubbed-away: a craftsman's sigil, and beneath it a tally of years. Someone worked this bench long before the shaft was sunk.

umbral-relief.name: an Umbral relief
umbral-relief.description: Carved deep into the smooth-cut wall: tall, slender figures with wide, lightless eyes, processing toward something the rockfall has long since buried. The work is patient and very old — whoever cut it was here ages before the first prospector ever sank a shaft.

trapdoor.name: a heavy trapdoor
trapdoor.description: A square of banded planks set flush into the shed floor, an iron pull-ring sunk in one edge. It is barred from above, not below — Vesper keeps what she keeps down there. Heave it open and a ladder drops into the dark of her practice cellar.

sealed-claim-door.name: a sealed mine-door
sealed-claim-door.description: The adit north of the road has been shut with a slab door of iron-banded timber, set into the hewn rock and barred with the reeve's lock. A board nailed across it reads, in the careful copy-hand of Fenn's office: CONDEMNED — WORKING CLOSED BY ORDER OF THE REEVE — NO ENTRY. The dark behind it has been left to whatever drove the prospectors out. Only the office key opens this.

ward-ring.name: a warding ring
ward-ring.description: A wide circle scored into the cellar floor and pinned at its cardinal points with stakes of raw glimmer, the lines chalked over and re-chalked until the stone is pale with them. Within it a loose knot of light is bound and cannot drift past the ring — a captive mark for a delver to throw spells at without bringing the whole Rim down around their ears.

pale-cluster.name: a cluster of pale mushrooms
pale-cluster.description: Pale, ivory-capped mushrooms crowd a damp seam of the wall, each breathing a weak, steady glow. It is enough to draw the grazers in out of the black to feed — and enough, just, to see them by.

silver-vein.name: a silver vein
silver-vein.description: A pale seam threads the dark rock, its colour distinct from the rust-red iron all around it — cooler, finer, harder-won. The ore runs thin and wants more effort than iron to loose, but what comes free is worth the swing.

umbral-pillar.name: an Umbral pillar
umbral-pillar.description: A squat, square-cut column of polished stone set into the floor of the cut — not natural, and not the prospectors' work either. Its four faces are scored with the same lightless-eye glyphs found on the old reliefs, cut with the same patient hand and the same indifference to whoever might one day read them. What it marked, or held, or meant, the rock keeps to itself.

river-shallows.name: the river shallows
river-shallows.description: The black water spreads thin and slow over a shelf of pale stone, barely shin-deep. Things move in it — the blind, drifting cave-fish that nose the current for whatever the dark lets fall. A patient hand and a baited line could take a few; the grubs the fungus-beds breed are the surest bait.

still-pool.name: a still pool
still-pool.description: Off the main race the river deepens into a broad, black pool, its surface unbroken but for the slow rings where something rises to feed below. The fish run larger and thicker down in the cold dark — good water for a baited line.

keepers-lake.name: the kept lake
keepers-lake.description: A broad, still lake held in a basin of worked stone, kept and stocked by the deep-folk and lit blue-green by the crop crowding its banks. Pale blind cave-fish hang thick in the cold shallows, untroubled and unfished but by a patient hand — the fat grubs the terraces breed are the surest bait.

umbral-garden-beds.name: the garden beds
umbral-garden-beds.description: Terraced beds of dark, damp earth climb the cavern wall, each one carefully furred with luminous fungus — not the wild witchglow of the deep tunnels but a tended, even crop, coaxed into neat rows that breathe a soft blue-green light. Someone has worked this ground a very long time, and works it still.

umbral-cistern.name: a stone cistern
umbral-cistern.description: A broad basin cut square from the floor catches a thread of clean water seeping clear through the rock above, drop by patient drop. The overflow is led off in a shallow worked channel that threads away among the beds. The water is dark and bitterly cold and looks fit to drink — kept so, like everything Mallki tends. It is what feeds the garden, and the garden feeds him.

umbral-stonebug-grounds.name: the grazing grounds
umbral-stonebug-grounds.description: The cavern floor lies thick with glowing moss, broad and untamed, climbing the lower walls and spilling between the stones. No furrows, no fences — just good feed left to grow, and the slow grey shapes that drift in out of the dark to crop it. The stonebugs come and go as they please; the moss only grows the faster for their grazing, and that easy balance has fed shell, meat, and tallow up to the hollow for longer than anyone can say.

thornbug-egg-sacks.name: a cluster of egg sacks
thornbug-egg-sacks.description: Wedged into a sheltered crook of the wall, a clutch of leathery egg sacks hangs in a dense, glistening cluster, each the size of a fist and translucent enough that a dark, curled shape can be made out coiled within. They give off no light of their own, only catching the cap-glow in a wet sheen, and a faint warmth rises off the mass where the heaviest of the herd keep their slow watch nearby. Nothing stirs in them yet.

umbral-coldlamp.name: a cold glimmer-lamp
umbral-coldlamp.description: A fist-sized knot of raw glimmer rests in a carved stone cradle, breathing a strong, blue-white light that throws no heat and no glare — bright enough to fill a hall, yet gentle enough not to pain deep-adapted eyes. The Umbrals light their halls this way, where surface folk would strike a torch and wince at their own flame. A sliding stone shutter can hood the glimmer or bare it.

glimmer-ward.name: a warding of glimmer-stakes
glimmer-ward.description: Lines scored deep into the worked stone and chalked pale, pinned at their turns with stakes of raw glimmer that breathe a low, steady blue-white light. It is the deep-folk's oldest guard — a bound edge of light set against the dark and the things the dark makes, holding at the very limit of the kept places. Within it the dark does not gather; beyond it, it waits.

broken-ward.name: a broken warding
broken-ward.description: The twin of the guard at the bridge's end, and long dead. The same lines were scored into this threshold stone once and pinned with stakes of raw glimmer — but the stakes are drawn or snuffed, sockets gaping empty, the chalked lines scuffed to grey ghosts underfoot, and where the light once held the dark has come back in and settled. Whatever the deep-folk set this edge against, it is on this side of it now. The warding gives no light at all.

grave-niches.name: a wall of grave-niches
grave-niches.description: The worked rock is cut floor to ceiling with row upon row of shallow niches, each just large enough to fold a body into — the deep-folk's old way of keeping their dead close, wrapped and set in the wall to lie in the dark they came from. Most gape empty now, their keepings long since stood up and shuffled off. A few still hold their wrapped shapes, black and stiff and silent; and here and there a deeper niche holds nothing the eye can name, only a dark that seems to fold back further than the stone should allow.

chullpa-tower.name: a chullpa, a tower of the dead
chullpa-tower.description: A squat tower of fitted stone stands among the graves, taller than a man and windowless but for a single low door facing the dark — a house raised for the honoured dead, that they might keep watch over their kin below from within it rather than beneath the ground. The deep-folk built it with the same close, seamless craft as the sanctuary far above, and time has not loosened a stone of it. But the low door is a mouth of pure black, and nothing that should be keeping watch keeps it now.

weeping-moss-curtain.name: a curtain of weeping chasm-moss
weeping-moss-curtain.description: A mass of bioluminescent moss clings to the ceiling and upper rock above the river, trailing long, hair-fine tendrils into the damp air. Each one beads with cold moisture and catches drifting organic matter — a slow, patient filter in the dark. The glow it gives is faint and blue-white, barely enough to see by, but unmistakable. Pull a clump free and it comes away without resistance, tendrils and all.

weeping-moss-fringe.name: a fringe of weeping chasm-moss
weeping-moss-fringe.description: Where the seep-water beads and falls, a thin fringe of bioluminescent moss has taken to the wet rock, trailing a few hair-fine tendrils into the dark. It glows the faintest blue-white — a stray outrider of the great curtains that hang in the deep river-caves below. Pull a clump free and it comes away easily, tendrils and all.

vesper-field-journal.name: Vesper's field journal
vesper-field-journal.description:
A worn leather notebook left open on a camp table shoved against the cellar wall, its pages puckered with mineral dust and damp. Most of the book is dense alchemical notation; somewhere past the middle the columns break off and the hand loosens into field observation.

— *The Umbral who tends the hollow near the paqcha — the waterfall — showed me the far relief today. I have counted seventeen distinct eye-glyph variants. Not decoration. A system.*

— *A word I have now heard from three separate individuals: ukhu-pacha. Spoken without particular weight, the way one says 'the cellar' or 'the floor below'. I suspect it names not a place but a condition — the interior world, the depth beneath depth. They do not seem to consider it remarkable.*

— *I asked Mallki about the grey ones — the wasted things in the lowest passages. He was quiet for longer than usual. Eventually: suti mana kan. I have written it as I heard it; my Umbral is still poor. I believe it means something like: the name is not. Or: they no longer have a name. He did not elaborate and I did not ask again.*

— *They watch the shaft-work. Every day, one or two of them standing at the gallery mouth. Not obstructing. Not speaking. Only watching. I do not know what to make of it.*

*The last entry trails off mid-sentence. The facing page is blank.*

shrine-font.name: a carved stone font
shrine-font.description: A shallow basin cut into the shrine floor below the relief, catching a thin thread of the fall that seeps clean through the rock. The water is clear and bitterly cold — the Umbrals' offering-water, kept apart from the black lake beyond. It looks safe to drink.

claims-ledger.name: the chained ledger
claims-ledger.description: A great canvas-bound ledger chained to the counter, swollen with years of entries in Fenn's cramped, exact hand: descent-permits, dig-licenses, fees paid, and the name of every soul who went below. Whole columns are struck through — came up and moved on, or simply never came up. Against the quiet ones Fenn pencils a date and waits; against the quiet-too-long he writes nothing at all, only underlines the name and leaves it open. One entry near the back — WENDER, M., fourth level — is underlined twice.

notice-board.name: the notice board
notice-board.description: A plank board by the door-flap, pinned edge to edge with postings in Fenn's careful copy-hand — standing bounties the traders pay him to broker, supply orders from the row, and the reeve's own notices under his lead seal. Each one is squared and priced; Fenn charges by the word. Read it over and take what work suits you.

cellar-hatch.name: a low cellar hatch
cellar-hatch.description: A plank hatch set into the floorboards behind the bar, worn smooth where Maeve lifts it day in, day out. A faint, animal reek drifts up through the seams — and now and then a scrabble of claws on stone. She is quick to wave a delver off it: nothing down there, dear, only stores. Heave it open and a short ladder drops into the cellar.

camp-lamp.name: a prospector's oil lamp
camp-lamp.description: A battered brass lamp left standing on a flat stone, its reservoir still a third full of oil gone thick with the cold. Whoever camped here carried it down from the surface and left it behind — too heavy, or too far gone to matter. A little wheel on the side feeds the wick up or down. Wound down, it sits dark; coax it up and it will burn a while yet.

camp-firepit.name: a cooking fire
camp-firepit.description: A ring of blackened stones holds a bed of cold ash and the charred ends of timber hauled down piece by piece from far above. A bent iron grille is propped across it and a soot-caked pot left upturned beside the ring. The fire is long dead, but the makings are all here — strike it up and there is room to cook a catch over the flames.

camp-bench.name: a makeshift alchemist's bench
camp-bench.description: A plank laid across two stacked supply crates, crowded with the tools a delver could carry and no more: a clay burner, a nest of chipped glass, a mortar worn pale at the rim. It is a poor cousin to the benches of the row above, knocked together for a season's work and never struck — but the coils are sound and it will still hold a reaction together.

camp-seep.name: a catch-basin of seep-water
camp-seep.description: A scooped hollow in the rock, walled round with a low rim of packed clay to catch the clean water that threads down the wall drop by drop. The prospectors who held this camp set it here to spare themselves the climb to the lake. The water is dark and cold and clean enough — a place to drink and gather yourself before the dark below.

umbral-glyph-panel.name: a hidden glyph-panel
umbral-glyph-panel.description: A slab of the gallery wall set so cunningly into the relief that only a searching eye catches the seam — a panel the deep-folk meant to be found by their own and no one else. Behind it the carving changes utterly: not the clawed ruin of the failing work outside but the old, sure hand, cut deep and clean. It shows a stair going down past warding-marks into a kept place — a sanctuary sealed against the dark and the things the dark unmakes — and the deep-folk as they once were at the foot of it, gathered and waiting. Whatever broke the watchers above, this was carved before it, by hands that still hoped. Loose stones and the dust of ages lie at its foot.

glimmer-vein.name: a glimmer vein
glimmer-vein.description: A seam of raw glimmer threads the rock, cold mineral light caught in the stone in dull blue-white veins, too faint to push back the dark but unmistakable to a delver's eye. This is the richer find the Rush came down for: worked loose with a patient arm, the seam gives up a whole glimmer crystal before it runs thin — and it is a long, hard while before it is worth the swing again.

glimmer-seam.name: a thin glimmer seam
glimmer-seam.description: A hairline seam of raw glimmer threads the broken face, cold blue-white light caught in the rock in flecks and thread-thin veins — not the fist-thick lodes the Rush came down dreaming of, but the picked-over shallows near the Rim still hold this much. A patient arm works the seam for a scatter of loose shards, and now and then, where the rock has not yet shed it, a whole small crystal comes free.

prospectors-rigging.name: old prospectors' climbing-rigging
prospectors-rigging.description: Iron pitons hammered deep into the flue at arm's-reach intervals, a knotted climbing-line gone stiff and grey strung between them, and the rotted remains of a rope-sling cinched to the rock. Prospectors rigged this fissure as a shortcut — a fast, ugly climb between the river workings far above and the deep below, cut and equipped before the warren went bad. The line still holds, more or less; the pitons are sound. It was a way down for those in a hurry to be rich, and a way up for those who lived to use it. Whoever last came down it did not climb back out.

umbral-forge.name: an Umbral glimmer-hearth
umbral-forge.description: Not a coal forge like the surface keeps, but a squat hearth of fitted stone with a cradle of raw glimmer at its heart, breathing a low blue-white heat that throws no smoke. A worked-stone anvil stands beside it, its face inlaid with the rounded glyphs the deep-folk cut into everything they make. Mallki works chitin, glimmer, and bound steel here in the slow, patient way of his people — and it will serve a delver who has the knowing of a recipe and the makings to hand.

fulgurite-vein.name: a seam of fulgurite
fulgurite-vein.description: A band of dark, glassy stone runs through the rock here, fused hard and smooth where the deep's old fires once licked the wall — fulgurite, in the deep-folk's word for it. It throws a delver's lamp back in a dull, oily sheen and breaks away in keen-edged shards under a patient arm. The Umbral come for it; what they make of it glows, though the raw stone never does.

glimmerglass-kiln.name: an Umbral kiln
glimmerglass-kiln.description: A round, low-domed kiln of fitted stone with a cradle of raw glimmer banked at its heart, breathing the same smokeless blue-white heat the deep-folk coax from everything they work. It is hot enough to bring fulgurite to a fusing-glow without shattering it, and patient hands use it to bed glimmer into the glass — folding captive light into a dark pane that holds it for good. A delver with the knowing of a recipe and the makings to hand may work it the same way.

caged-gate.name: a caged gate
caged-gate.description: The whole southern arch is closed off with a wall of forged iron bars — not a door so much as the front of a cage, each bar as thick as a pick-haft, set deep into holes drilled in the living rock and strapped with cross-bands at knee and shoulder height. A gate is built into the middle of it, hinged and heavy, wound about with chain and shut with a prospector's lock gone orange with the wet. Whoever raised it hauled the iron down five floors to do it, and meant it to hold: the bars are set to keep something in, not delvers out. The warm wind pours through regardless, beading the metal, and far down the passage beyond glows that pale, steady wash of light that no one is carrying.

gate-warning.name: a painted warning
gate-warning.description: Across the cross-bands of the gate, in whitewash gone grey and laid on thick with a rag, someone painted letters a hand tall: DO NOT ENTER. Below it, smaller, in a different and shakier hand: IT IS NOT WORTH IT. THE LIGHT IS A LIE. Below that, a tally — four strokes and a fifth across them, and two strokes more — the way prospectors count their dead.

verge-niche.name: a cut lamp-shelf
verge-niche.description: A niche cut square into the cave wall at shoulder height — not worn, cut, the chisel-strokes still crisp — its ceiling smoke-blacked where a lamp has sat and burned. Below it the rusted stub of an iron stake juts from the rock, and beside that a chalk blaze has faded to a ghost. Someone works this edge of the browse, and has for a long time; whatever they carry, they carry it down.

gallery-sealed-face.name: the sealed west face
gallery-sealed-face.description: A few paces on west the gallery simply stops: a fall of rock brought down across the full bore, shored with squared timber so that no more of it can move. Whoever drove this tunnel meant to go further — the dressed walls run right up to the rubble and vanish into it — and someone, later, made very sure nothing would come of it. The timbers are newer than the fall.

den-door.name: a heavy plank door
den-door.description: A door of heavy squared planks set into a dressed frame in the gallery's south wall, hung on leather hinges kept supple with lamp-oil. There is no lock and no bar — down past the browse and the squeeze and a tunnel no natural force made, whoever hung it plainly reckons the dark does the gatekeeping. A faint warmth of lamplight breathes through the seams.

den-lamps.name: the Graveworker's lamps
den-lamps.description: Oil lamps in fitted iron brackets, spaced with the same measured care as the tunnel's timber sets, every wick trimmed and every reservoir topped. They are never allowed to go out — the work needs light, and the man who works here plainly reckons the dark can have every room but his. Even when the deep's black tide comes flooding in, these burn on.

graveworker-journal.name: a working journal
graveworker-journal.description: A thick journal lies open on the bench, written in a small, steady hand — no ravings, just work: wire gauges and joint diagrams, tallies of shard spent per subject, a thornbug's leg sketched rebuilt in coil. One margin, underlined twice: 'the wire does not tire. neither do they.' A later page, calmer still: 'flesh is scaffolding. bone is the instrument.' The most recent entry is a shopping list.
